commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Sabbaths, every seventh year, Leviticus xxv. 2., and xxvi. 43., and Jeremias xxv. 11., and xxix. 10.
Expired: not that the earth was quite neglected, all that time, from the third year of Joakim, the year of the world 3398 till 3468. (Calmet)
But it was almost: particularly after the murder of Godolias, (Menochius) the public worship was at a stand (Haydock)
During this fifth age, the true religion subsisted invariable, not only in the kingdom of Juda, but also in that of Israel. See Worthington. (Haydock)
